label: SOC Analyst
description: Monitors security events, investigates alerts, and responds to incidents in a security operations center. Typically Tier 1-3 progression.

label: Penetration Tester
description: Conducts authorized offensive security assessments against networks, applications, and systems to identify and exploit vulnerabilities.

label: DFIR Analyst
description: Investigates security incidents through digital forensics, memory analysis, and incident response. Works in corporate IR teams, consulting firms, or law enforcement.

label: Cloud Security Engineer
description: Designs, implements, and operates security controls in cloud environments. Works across AWS, Azure, GCP, or multi-cloud infrastructure.

label: Application Security Engineer
description: Secures software throughout the development lifecycle — from code review and threat modeling through penetration testing and DevSecOps integration.

label: CTI Analyst
description: Produces and consumes cyber threat intelligence to inform defensive decisions. Profiles threat actors, analyzes TTPs, and produces intelligence products.

label: Security Architect
description: Designs enterprise security architectures, defines security standards and patterns, and leads the security aspects of major technology programs.

label: CISO / Security Director
description: Executive responsible for the overall security posture of an organization — strategy, governance, risk management, and team leadership.

label: GRC Analyst / Risk Manager
description: Manages governance, risk, and compliance programs — conducting risk assessments, managing audits, and ensuring regulatory compliance.

label: IAM Engineer
description: Implements and manages identity and access management systems including directories, SSO, MFA, PAM, and identity governance platforms.

label: Privacy Professional / DPO
description: Manages organizational privacy compliance, serves as Data Protection Officer, implements privacy programs, and advises on regulatory requirements.

label: Vulnerability Management Engineer
description: Operates and manages vulnerability scanning programs, prioritizes findings, tracks remediation, and manages the VM platform and tooling.

label: OT / ICS Security Specialist
description: Secures operational technology and industrial control systems in critical infrastructure, manufacturing, energy, and utilities environments.

label: AI Security Specialist
description: Secures AI systems, conducts AI red teaming, implements AI governance, and advises on AI risk management. An emerging role as AI becomes a core attack surface.

label: Web App Pentester
description: Specializes in identifying and exploiting web application vulnerabilities through black-box and white-box testing.

label: Exploit Developer
description: Develops working exploits for software vulnerabilities and performs vulnerability research — binary exploitation, reverse engineering, mitigation bypass.

label: Red Team Operator
description: Conducts full-scope adversary simulation — C2 infrastructure, EDR evasion, persistence, lateral movement, and objective-based operations.

label: AD / Enterprise Pentester
description: Specializes in internal network and Active Directory penetration testing — credential attacks, privilege escalation, and domain compromise.

label: Cloud Pentester
description: Specializes in offensive security assessments against cloud infrastructure across AWS, Azure, and GCP environments.

label: Mobile Security Tester
description: Specializes in security assessment of Android and iOS mobile applications — static and dynamic analysis, reverse engineering, and API testing.

label: IoT / Hardware Pentester
description: Performs security assessments on embedded systems and IoT devices — firmware extraction, UART/JTAG/SPI exploitation, and RF attacks.

label: Wireless Security Specialist
description: Specializes in wireless network security assessment — WPA2/WPA3 attacks, enterprise wireless, rogue access points, and Bluetooth attacks.

label: Social Engineer / Physical Pentester
description: Conducts human-based security assessments — phishing simulations, vishing, physical intrusion testing, and OSINT-based pretexting.

label: Malware Analyst / Reverse Engineer
description: Analyzes malicious software through static and dynamic analysis to understand behavior, extract IOCs, and develop detection signatures.

label: Digital Forensics Examiner
description: Conducts forensic examinations of digital evidence for law enforcement, legal proceedings, and corporate investigations.

label: Mobile Forensics Analyst
description: Extracts and analyzes data from mobile devices including smartphones, tablets, and wearables.

label: IR Consultant
description: Leads and manages cyber incident response engagements — detection, containment, eradication, recovery, and breach investigation.

label: Network Forensics Analyst
description: Investigates security incidents through network traffic analysis — PCAP analysis, protocol reconstruction, and network-based threat hunting.

label: Detection Engineer
description: Builds and maintains detection logic for SIEM and EDR platforms — Sigma rules, KQL queries, SPL, and YARA signatures.

label: Threat Hunter
description: Proactively searches for attacker presence through hypothesis-driven investigation and intelligence-led hunting before automated alerts fire.

label: SIEM / Platform Engineer
description: Builds, operates, and scales SIEM and security data platform infrastructure — ingestion pipelines, log parsing, and platform architecture.

label: Network Security Engineer
description: Designs, implements, and operates network security infrastructure — firewalls, IPS/IDS, VPN, and NGFW policy management.

label: Zero Trust / SASE Specialist
description: Designs and implements Zero Trust network access, SASE frameworks, and cloud-delivered network security.

label: DevSecOps Engineer
description: Integrates security into software delivery pipelines — SAST, DAST, SCA, container security, IaC security, and secrets management.

label: Container / K8s Security Engineer
description: Secures containerized workloads and Kubernetes clusters — pod security, RBAC, network policies, runtime security, and image scanning.

label: Cloud Security Architect
description: Designs cloud security architecture at a strategic level — reference architectures, governance frameworks, and multi-cloud security strategy.

label: IT Auditor
description: Conducts independent assessments of IT controls, information security programs, and regulatory compliance.

label: IT / Cyber Risk Manager
description: Identifies, quantifies, and manages IT and cyber risk — risk assessments, treatment plans, quantification (FAIR), and senior reporting.

label: BC / DR Manager
description: Develops and manages business continuity, disaster recovery, and organizational resilience programs.

label: TPRM Manager
description: Manages vendor and third-party risk programs — SIG questionnaire-based assessments, vendor tiering, and continuous monitoring.

label: Data Security / DLP Engineer
description: Implements and manages data security controls — data classification, DLP policy, data governance, and information protection technologies.

label: SOC Manager
description: Manages security operations center teams and programs — shift management, analyst development, SLA management, and SOC governance.

label: Security Program Manager
description: Manages information security programs at the organizational level — project portfolio management, vendor management, and budget planning.

label: Security Awareness Manager
description: Designs and manages security awareness programs and human risk management — phishing simulation, culture measurement, and behavior change.
